title: cistus × nigricans subsp. grosii
intro: Cistus × nigricans subsp. grosii, also known as the 'Gros's Rockrose' or 'Gros's Cistus', is a hybrid plant species within the genus Cistus, which is a type of flowering shrub native to the Iberian Peninsula. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Cistus × nigricans subsp. grosii include its potential to soothe skin irritations, reduce inflammation, and support immune function. Herbal preparations of this plant are often made into teas, infusions, or topical creams, allowing for a range of applications in traditional medicine.
